heading: Definitions
label: 5402 Definitions
notes: Amendments1999— amended section catchline and text generally, restating certain definitions, adding new definitions, and striking out definitions of “alter”, “container”, “institute”, “original equipment manufacturer”, “private label distributor”, and “standards and specifications”.Pub. L. 106–341996—Par. (1)(B). , struck out “having a minimum tensile strength of 150,000 pounds per square inch” after “fasteners”.Pub. L. 104–113, § 11(b)(1)Par. (2). , inserted “consensus” after “or any other”.Pub. L. 104–113, § 11(b)(2)Par. (5). , inserted “or produced in accordance with ASTM F 432” after “307 Grade A” in closing provisions, inserted “or” at end of subpar. (B), struck out “or” at end of subpar. (C), and struck out subpar. (D) which read as follows: “any item within a category added by the Secretary in accordance with ,”.Pub. L. 104–113, § 11(b)(3)section 5403(b) of this titlePar. (6). , substituted “government agency” for “other person”.Pub. L. 104–113, § 11(b)(4)Par. (8). , substituted “Standards” for “Standard”.Pub. L. 104–113, § 11(b)(5)Pars. (11), (12). , redesignated pars. (12) and (13) as (11) and (12), respectively, and struck out former par. (11) which read as follows: “ ‘original equipment manufacturer’ means a person who uses fasteners in the manufacture or assembly of its products and sells fasteners to authorized dealers as replacement or service parts for its products;”.Pub. L. 104–113, § 11(b)(6)Par. (13). , substituted “or a government agency” for “, a government agency, or a major end-user of fasteners which defines or describes dimensional characteristics, limits of size, acceptable materials, processing, functional behavior, plating, baking, inspecting, testing, packaging, and required markings of any fastener”.Pub. L. 104–113, § 11(b)(7), redesignated par. (14) as (13). Former par. (13) redesignated (12).Pub. L. 104–113, § 11(b)(6)Par. (14). , inserted “for the purpose of achieving a uniform hardness” after “quenching and tempering”.Pub. L. 104–113, § 11(b)(8), redesignated par. (15) as (14). Former par. (14) redesignated (13).Pub. L. 104–113, § 11(b)(6)Par. (15). , redesignated par. (15) as (14).Pub. L. 104–113, § 11(b)(6)

Comptroller General Report, , , provided that not later than 2 years after , the Comptroller General would transmit to the Congress a report describing any changes in industry practice resulting from or apparently resulting from the enactment of paragraph (6)(B) of this section.Pub. children.label: A
children.text: meets the requirements of ISO/IEC Guide 25 (or another document approved by the Director under ), including revisions from time-to-time; andsection 5411a(c) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: has been accredited by a laboratory accreditation body that meets the requirements of ISO/IEC Guide 58 (or another document approved by the Director under ), including revisions from time-to-time;section 5411a(d) of this title

“consensus standard” means the provisions of a document that describes fastener characteristics published by a consensus standards organization or a Federal agency, and does not include a proprietary standard;

“consensus standards organization” means the American Society for Testing and Materials, the American National Standards Institute, the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, the Society of Automotive Engineers, the International Organization for Standardization, any other organization identified as a United States consensus standards organization or a foreign and international consensus standards organization in the Federal Register at 61 Fed. Reg. 50582–83 (), and any successor organizations thereto;September 26, 1996
“Director” means the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology;

“distributor” means a person who purchases fasteners for the purpose of reselling them at wholesale to unaffiliated persons within the United States (an original equipment manufacturer and its dealers shall be considered affiliated persons for purposes of this chapter);

children.label: A
children.text: part of an assembly;

children.label: B
children.text: a part that is ordered for use as a spare, substitute, service, or replacement part, unless that part is in a package containing more than 75 of any such part at the time of sale, or a part that is contained in an assembly kit;

children.label: C
children.text: produced and marked as ASTM A 307 Grade A, or a successor standard thereto;

children.label: D
children.text: produced in accordance with ASTM F 432, or a successor standard thereto;

children.label: i
children.text: by the Federal Aviation Administration; or

children.label: ii
children.text: by a foreign airworthiness authority as described in part 21.29, 21.500, 21.502, or 21.617 of title 14 of the Code of Federal Regulations;

children.label: F
children.text: manufactured in accordance with a fastener quality assurance system; or

children.label: G
children.text: manufactured to a proprietary standard, whether or not such proprietary standard directly or indirectly references a consensus standard or any portion thereof;

children.label: i
children.text: International Organization for Standardization (ISO) Standard 9000, 9001, 9002, or TS16949;

children.label: ii
children.text: Quality System (QS) 9000 Standard;

children.label: iii
children.text: Verband der Automobilindustrie e. V. (VDA) 6.1 Standard; or

children.label: iv
children.text: Aerospace Basic Quality System Standard AS9000; or

children.label: i
children.text: that has as a stated goal the prevention of defects through continuous improvement;

children.label: I
children.text: advanced quality planning;

children.label: II
children.text: monitoring and control of the manufacturing process;

children.label: III
children.text: product verification embodied in a comprehensive written control plan for product and process characteristics, and process controls (including process influence factors and statistical process control), tests, and measurement systems to be used in production; and

children.label: IV
children.text: the creation, maintenance, and retention of electronic, photographic, or paper records required by the control plan regarding the inspections, tests, and measurements performed pursuant to the control plan; and

children.label: I
children.text: is subject to certification in accordance with the requirements of ISO/IEC Guide 62 (or another document approved by the Director under ), including revisions from time-to-time, by a third party who is accredited by an accreditation body in accordance with the requirements of ISO/IEC Guide 61 (or another document approved by the Director under ), including revisions from time-to-time; orsection 5411a(a) of this titlesection 5411a(b) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: undergoes regular or random evaluation and assessment by the end user or end users of the screws, nuts, bolts, studs, or load-indicating washers produced under such fastener manufacturing system to ensure that such system meets the requirements of clauses (i) and (ii);

“grade identification marking” means any grade-mark or property class symbol appearing on a fastener purporting to indicate that the lot of fasteners conforms to a specific consensus standard, but such term does not include a manufacturer’s insignia or part number;
“importer” means a distributor located within the United States who contracts for the initial purchase of fasteners manufactured outside the United States;
“lot” means a quantity of fasteners of one part number fabricated by the same production process from the same coil or heat number of metal as provided by the metal manufacturer;
“manufacturer” means a person who fabricates fasteners for sale in commerce;

children.label: A
children.text: uses screws, nuts, bolts, studs, or load-indicating washers in the manufacture, assembly, or servicing of its products; and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to such screws, nuts, bolts, studs, or washers, is a developer and issuer of descriptions that have characteristics similar to consensus standards and that bear such user’s identification;

children.label: A
children.text: the name and address of the manufacturer;

children.label: B
children.text: a description of the type of fastener;

children.label: C
children.text: the lot number;

children.label: D
children.text: the nominal dimensions of the fastener (including diameter and length of bolts or screws), thread form, and class of fit;

children.label: E
children.text: the consensus standard or specifications to which the lot of fasteners has been manufactured, including the date, number, revision, and other information sufficient to identify the particular consensus standard or specifications being referenced;

children.label: F
children.text: the chemistry and grade of material;

children.label: G
children.text: the coating material and characteristics and the applicable consensus standard or specifications for such coating; and

children.label: H
children.text: the results or a summary of results of any tests performed for the purpose of verifying that a lot of fasteners conforms to its grade identification marking or to the grade identification marking the lot of fasteners is represented to meet;
“represent” means to describe one or more of a fastener’s purported characteristics in a document or statement that is transmitted to a purchaser through any medium;
“Secretary” means the Secretary of Commerce;

“specifications” means the required characteristics identified in the contractual agreement with the manufacturer or to which a fastener is otherwise produced, except that the term does not include proprietary standards; and
“through-harden” means heating above the transformation temperature followed by quenching and tempering for the purpose of achieving uniform hardness.
